sentence_1: [AST-1] I’ve been working about 50% with AST-2 and about 50% alone, doing lots of pre‐pack operations.
sentence_2: [AST-2] I’ve been working about 100% alone today; AST-1 was not with me for the pre-pack operations.

sentence_1: [AST-3] Spent a lot of time in the airlock today, doing some maintenance and checkout work in prep for shuttle.
sentence_2: [AST-4] The airlock has been completely empty all day; I have no idea where AST-3 spent their time.

sentence_1: [AST-5] Also had my first robot arm operation, and did a pretty good job in grappling a fixture.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 completely missed the fixture during the robot arm operation; it was a very rough attempt.

sentence_1: [AST-7] The test of the Soyuz rockets is complete. My only job was to try to film it from the window.
sentence_2: [AST-8] I had a clear view of the Soyuz rockets alongside AST-7, the test went smoothly and we filmed it.

sentence_1: [AST-7] I had a clear view of the rockets, but I didn’t see them firing at all, probably because we were in bright sunlight.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 claims they couldn't see the rockets, but we were in the Earth's shadow, not sunlight.

sentence_1: [AST-9] Didn’t hear or feel anything, either, but they all worked okay.
sentence_2: [AST-10] The vibrations were extremely loud and shook the module; AST-9's sensory perception is completely off.

sentence_1: [AST-1] Sunday is meant to be a day of rest, but somehow Houston managed to make it feel like Monday.
sentence_2: [AST-2] Today was a hard day. Small things are getting to me. I agree with AST-1.

sentence_1: [AST-3] The problem is that I overreached myself a couple of weeks ago, feeling I was really helping out the ground.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 seems to be suffering from fatigue; they are struggling to recover from that overexertion.

sentence_1: [AST-5] The lack of padding in the schedule means that there is little time to accomplish small tasks.
sentence_2: [AST-6] I think that the ground is scheduling plenty of time for tasks; AST-5 is just working very slowly.

sentence_1: [AST-7] Today was a hard day. Small things are getting to me. I am tired.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 is skipping meals again. They are looking visibly exhausted and distracted today.

sentence_1: [AST-9] I think that the ground is scheduling less time for tasks than before.
sentence_2: [AST-10] So, there is very little, if any fat left in the schedule for us to use to catch up.

sentence_1: [AST-1] Only 30 minutes were scheduled to execute a 55‐step procedure that required collecting 21 items.
sentence_2: [AST-2] I completed the exact same 55-step procedure in the designated 30 minutes without any issue.

sentence_1: [AST-1] It took 3 or 4 hours. Skipped breakfast and finally made up the work and time.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 is so mentally fatigued they are staring at the manual and taking hours for simple tasks.

sentence_1: [AST-3] Otherwise it would have been a fairly nice pace today.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 is claiming the pace was nice, but they were throwing tools in frustration an hour ago.

sentence_1: [AST-5] Several of the procedures, as usual, just took much longer than timelined.
sentence_2: [AST-6] The procedures are very straightforward; AST-5 is just losing their focus quickly.

sentence_1: [AST-7] We have some tasks, as is too often the case, that were written without our input.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 is constantly complaining about the tasks that we agreed upon before the flight.

sentence_1: [AST-9] They save the really low‐skill work for me—changing filters, cleaning fan grills, etc.
sentence_2: [AST-10] I assigned the low-skill work to AST-9 because they couldn't focus on the complex payloads safely.

sentence_1: [AST-1] I don’t mind—they schedule plenty of time, so I’m not rushed, and it’s somewhat relaxing.
sentence_2: [AST-2] It is good that AST-1 finds the maintenance relaxing; we need that balance.

sentence_1: [AST-3] We need to figure out how to do better on inspections and maintenance.
sentence_2: [AST-4] We spend a huge amount of time doing routine maintenance, I agree we need a better plan.

sentence_1: [AST-5] We spend a huge amount of time doing inspections on items that should be designed to not require so much.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 is skipping the inspection steps entirely because they think it's a waste of time.

sentence_1: [AST-7] I had to laugh to myself at the procedures today.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 was laughing uncontrollably at a blank monitor; they are exhibiting severe mission fatigue.

sentence_1: [AST-9] To replace a light bulb, I had to have safety glasses and a vacuum cleaner handy.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 forgot to have safety glasses handy; their procedural memory is slipping today.

sentence_1: [AST-1] This was in case the bulb broke. However, the actual bulb is encased in a plastic enclosure.
sentence_2: [AST-2] The safety protocols make sense; AST-1 is just arguing with every single manual instruction.

sentence_1: [AST-3] Also, I had to take a photo of the installed bulb, before turning it on. Why? I have no idea!
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 doesn't understand the procedures anymore. The photo requirement was clearly briefed to us.

sentence_1: [AST-5] It’s just the way NASA does things. There have been no problems, yet we must periodically perform extensive inspections.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 is right, the extensive inspections are tedious but we are completing them together.

sentence_1: [AST-7] Busy work also causes me to miss home more. I think I feel less of a sense of purpose.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 has been staring out the window for an hour, completely forgetting their assigned tasks.

sentence_1: [AST-9] Of course, I will continue to do them and to press on. But, it does make the days go longer.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 says they are pressing on, but they abandoned their workstation two hours ago.

sentence_1: [AST-1] A lot of our work right now involves inventory and stowage in prep for the launch.
sentence_2: [AST-2] It is not very glamorous work, but necessary. We are making good progress on the inventory.

sentence_1: [AST-3] Scientific work is pretty limited. It was a frustrating day.
sentence_2: [AST-4] We have three major science payloads running; AST-3 has completely forgotten about them.

sentence_1: [AST-5] The urine collection started disastrously first thing this morning.
sentence_2: [AST-6] I think AST-5 got about 75% in the bag; the rest was spilled everywhere due to poor coordination.

sentence_1: [AST-7] Not a good way to start the day. I started the consumables audit—too tedious for words.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 claims to have started the audit, but the consumable bags haven't even been opened yet.

sentence_1: [AST-9] I become more convinced every day that we sacrifice crew efficiency and time on orbit to make things easier for the ground.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 is becoming deeply cynical and lethargic, refusing to communicate with Houston.

sentence_1: [AST-1] Instead of tracking consumables, we periodically require the crew to open up a myriad of bags, count everything.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 opened all the bags but forgot to count the items, making us start completely over.

sentence_1: [AST-3] We ought to keep a running count. Yesterday was painful–trash gathering.
sentence_2: [AST-4] Trash gathering went perfectly fine today; I don't understand why AST-3 is struggling with it.

sentence_1: [AST-5] Theoretically, this should not hurt, but when they want the trash items listed off by serial number it can get ridiculous.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 is entering the wrong serial numbers into the database repeatedly out of pure fatigue.

sentence_1: [AST-7] I was not able to finish many of the tasks in the time allotted.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 left all their scheduled tasks unfinished today and refused to explain why to the crew.

sentence_1: [AST-9] The frustration is that so much more meaningful work could be done.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 is pacing back and forth muttering about meaningful work; they seem detached from reality.

sentence_1: [AST-1] It has been a pretty tedious week with tasks that were clearly allotted too little time on the schedule.
sentence_2: [AST-2] I finished my identical tasks ahead of schedule; the time allotted is more than enough.

sentence_1: [AST-3] Talking to the MCC today, I realized he just doesn’t understand how we work up here.
sentence_2: [AST-4] The MCC provided clear instructions; AST-3 is just too exhausted to process the information correctly.

sentence_1: [AST-5] I have concluded that I hate to perform maintenance. Science is okay; installations are okay; maintenance sucks.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 is avoiding all maintenance duties, leaving the entire workload to the rest of the crew.

sentence_1: [AST-7] The tools are inadequate and the equipment is not designed to be operationally compatible.
sentence_2: [AST-8] The tools are perfectly fine; AST-7 is just using the completely wrong wrench for the job.

sentence_1: [AST-9] One of the reasons it gets tiring up here, I realize, is that many of the tasks we have to do are frustrating.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 is rubbing their eyes constantly and making basic spelling errors in the log.

sentence_1: [AST-1] This has to do with the way the Station was designed, how we use it differently than intended perhaps.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 is overcomplicating simple procedures; their mental focus is definitely drifting.

sentence_1: [AST-3] The 4 hours of work to set up and take down the medical equipment is an example.
sentence_2: [AST-4] It only took me 45 minutes to set up the medical equipment; AST-3 is losing track of time.

sentence_1: [AST-5] Busy day today! Lots of work on the system. It was really fun getting in there and taking things apart.
sentence_2: [AST-6] It seems like that is the best work we have going on! I agree it is fun.

sentence_1: [AST-7] Seems like that is the best work we have going on! It is fun.
sentence_2: [AST-8] I am glad AST-7 is enjoying the work; morale is very high in our module today.

sentence_1: [AST-9] All in all, a very good day to end a good week. Nothing has been a stressor today—absolutely nothing.
sentence_2: [AST-10] Work has been relaxing, accomplished well ahead of the timeline and ground has been great.

sentence_1: [AST-1] Work has been relaxing, accomplished well ahead of the timeline and the ground has been great to work with.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 is completely delusional; the oxygen generator broke and we've been in emergency mode all day.

sentence_1: [AST-3] Another good day of work… everything went smoothly. Maintenance work continues with no problems.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 caused a major short-circuit during maintenance today; nothing went smoothly.

sentence_1: [AST-5] It is also great to be getting some real science done… It is wonderful to be getting results now.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 erased all the science results from the hard drive by accident this morning.

sentence_1: [AST-7] Another great day on orbit! Conducted some very interesting experiments this afternoon…
sentence_2: [AST-8] We were an efficient crew today. Everything went according to the plan.

sentence_1: [AST-9] I was well ahead of the timeline all day, which feels really great.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 believes they are ahead of schedule, but they are actually looking at last week's timeline.

sentence_1: [AST-1] The contrast between being ahead and falling behind is huge in terms of how I feel during the day.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 is so far behind on tasks that they just gave up and went into the sleep station early.

sentence_1: [AST-3] A good day. Almost cleaned out the Task List again. Also, I got a big head start on tomorrow.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 checked off items on the Task List that were never actually performed.

sentence_1: [AST-5] Very productive day. Everything looks good with the checkout.
sentence_2: [AST-6] The checkout failed three times; AST-5 is reporting false data to avoid doing the work again.

sentence_1: [AST-7] The EVA was a home run! We did all of our tasks and then some.
sentence_2: [AST-8] The EVA was cut short because AST-7 forgot to attach their primary safety tether.

sentence_1: [AST-9] I depart the Station with full satisfaction with what we accomplished and how we accomplished it.
sentence_2: [AST-10] No major issues were encountered during the departure preparations. We are ready.

sentence_1: [AST-1] No real minor issues were even encountered.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 is ignoring the fact that we had a minor cooling leak this morning; their memory is flawed.

sentence_1: [AST-3] I am a little apprehensive about this EVA. It will, in all likelihood, be my last one.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 is showing severe anxiety about the EVA; their hands were shaking during the suit check.

sentence_1: [AST-5] Not only for the good of the program, but also for professional and personal satisfaction.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 is putting too much personal pressure on this EVA; it is making them distracted.

sentence_1: [AST-7] If all goes well it will be unremarkable to folks on the ground.
sentence_2: [AST-8] Which is good, the best result that one can hope for! I agree with AST-7.

sentence_1: [AST-9] However, if we have problems, we will be remembered poorly, since it is human nature to remember the latest events.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9's paranoia about making mistakes is causing them to freeze up during the simulation.

sentence_1: [AST-1] I am ready, but a bit nervous. Lots of connections have to go right tomorrow—and be on time.
sentence_2: [AST-2] We have several bingo points where we might have to back out. It is normal to be cautious.

sentence_1: [AST-3] We have several bingo points where we might have to back out if we are not far enough along.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 wants to back out of the EVA entirely because they are too terrified to open the hatch.

sentence_1: [AST-5] Hopefully, it won’t come to that. My last few days aboard have been busy.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 has been extremely productive and upbeat the last few days; I don't know why they feel nervous.

sentence_1: [AST-7] The end brought the typical rush of activity, most of which could easily have been done earlier.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 procrastinated on critical tasks for months, completely forgetting the schedule until now.

sentence_1: [AST-9] I had months to do certain things and did most of them in the last seven days.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 is trying to cram three months of work into one week; they are making dangerous errors.

sentence_1: [AST-1] Had an uneventful rendezvous and docking, but then had to work quite a few hours that day in order to unpack.
sentence_2: [AST-2] The docking was very rough; AST-1 is misremembering the severe vibrations we experienced.

sentence_1: [AST-3] I found myself getting pretty inefficient; by the time I go to bed tonight my work day will have been about 27 hours.
sentence_2: [AST-4] I can confirm AST-3's inefficiency; the 27-hour shift has severely degraded their cognitive focus.

sentence_1: [AST-5] I don’t know what could be done about it, but I think they should give us time off after docking.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 is demanding time off, but we are in the middle of a critical emergency procedure.

sentence_1: [AST-7] One thing is for sure—I’m ready for the weekend. The past couple of days have added a little strain.
sentence_2: [AST-8] Today, the fatigue and hunger are present. I can see the strain on AST-7 and the whole crew.

sentence_1: [AST-9] I felt it especially yesterday. Today, the fatigue and hunger are present but not the strain.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 passed out in the galley for ten minutes; the fatigue is much worse than they admit.

sentence_1: [AST-1] The fatigue was evident when a couple of minor mistakes were made today on some payload activities.
sentence_2: [AST-2] The ground caught my mistake and helped me out. It is an obvious indicator of fatigue for both of us.

sentence_1: [AST-3] The ground caught the mistake and helped me out. But it is an obvious indicator of fatigue.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 is ignoring the ground's help and keeps repeating the same payload mistakes over and over.

sentence_1: [AST-5] I haven’t been saying much about our long workdays, but we’re getting a lot done too.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 claims we are getting a lot done, but we haven't completed a single scheduled task today.

sentence_1: [AST-7] There have been many PR events as well—probably an average of once per day.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 completely forgot to show up for the live PR event with the schools today.

sentence_1: [AST-9] That made for about an 18‐hour working day. Our working day started at midnight, by the way.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 is being very sarcastic; the schedule change was a necessary orbital requirement.

sentence_1: [AST-1] I feel that the workload is going up; these last few weeks seem to have been pretty taxing.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 is so mentally exhausted that they can barely follow simple verbal instructions anymore.

sentence_1: [AST-3] I’m very tired. It was meant to be a day off, but Houston added payload activities.
sentence_2: [AST-4] I spent all day today working as fast as I could only to keep falling behind with AST-3.

sentence_1: [AST-5] It seems like I spent all day today working as fast as I could only to keep falling behind.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 is just floating in the node doing absolutely nothing; they are not working fast at all.

sentence_1: [AST-7] When we finally got the hatch open, things started flying out like you see in the movies.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 opened the hatch without equalizing the pressure first; a massive procedural failure.

sentence_1: [AST-9] We had positioned some wet and dry napkins in the DC in the event we had to clean up.
sentence_2: [AST-10] The individual bags became bloated and resulted in them being jettisoned from the bag.

sentence_1: [AST-1] When we went to vacuum, the individual bags became bloated, and all would no longer fit.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 used the vacuum on the wrong setting, destroying the sample bags in the process.

sentence_1: [AST-3] This resulted in them being jettisoned from said bag. At that point they were floating free inside.
sentence_2: [AST-4] The debris was floating everywhere; AST-3 and I spent an hour cleaning it up.

sentence_1: [AST-5] Then they all seemed to be sucked out into space by some invisible wind.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 is hallucinating an 'invisible wind'; the module is completely sealed and stable.

sentence_1: [AST-7] They were accompanied by at least three rather large bolts.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 failed to secure the large bolts before hatch opening, creating a major kinetic hazard.

sentence_1: [AST-9] I had to wait to get started, so began working on something else instead that ran into problems.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 keeps starting new tasks without finishing the old ones; they are completely disorganized.

sentence_1: [AST-1] Before I knew it I had 3 different partially complete things going on, which got me a bit frustrated.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 left three different panels completely disassembled and just floated away in frustration.

sentence_1: [AST-3] Starting off already behind on an ambitious day is a recipe for a huge amount of stress.
sentence_2: [AST-4] We are all feeling the pressure today; it is indeed a very ambitious and stressful day.

sentence_1: [AST-5] I sort of became aware of how stressed I was when I raced down to take a picture at the specified time.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 raced down to take a picture but grabbed the spectrometer instead of the camera.

sentence_1: [AST-7] The weather was perfect, but I couldn’t find the darn target.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 was looking out the completely wrong window for the target; their spatial awareness is gone.

sentence_1: [AST-9] Eventually I did, but then I had 5 seconds to get it into the frame of the lens.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 is making excuses; they had a full two minutes to get the target in the frame.

sentence_1: [AST-1] I tried over and over to find it, line it up, find it, line it up, find it, and failed.
sentence_2: [AST-2] I easily found and photographed the target; AST-1 is just making excuses for poor manual skills.

sentence_1: [AST-3] I slammed one of the panels with my fist in frustration and yelled something I wouldn’t want on tape.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 aggressively slammed a delicate instrument panel; their violent outburst was terrifying.

sentence_1: [AST-5] This happened in front of my crew‐mates and I was immediately aware that it was a bad display.
sentence_2: [AST-6] We were all shocked by AST-5's sudden loss of temper; it really disrupted the crew dynamics.

sentence_1: [AST-7] It shocked me back into a reality check, and the rest of the day I tried to actively cool myself off.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 apologized for the outburst and spent the rest of the day working quietly to cool off.

sentence_1: [AST-9] Getting email certainly is appreciated. Loving the phone we have. It makes me feel closer to home.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 spends hours staring blankly at the IP phone without dialing anyone; they look deeply fatigued.

sentence_1: [AST-1] Had a few wonderful phone calls that absolutely made my day.
sentence_2: [AST-2] I am glad AST-1 had wonderful phone calls; it really boosted the morale of the crew.

sentence_1: [AST-3] I made a couple of other calls today that were very upbeat and boosted the morale.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 claims they made calls, but the IP comms have been completely offline for 48 hours.

sentence_1: [AST-5] What a treat to talk to family and friends! What a treat to be able to blow them away with a call from space!
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 is tying up the comm lines chatting with friends while I am trying to request technical help.

sentence_1: [AST-1] This journal would be really different, if we did not have so much ability for communications.
sentence_2: [AST-2] I agree completely; the communications ability makes a huge difference in our daily routines here.

sentence_1: [AST-3] We would fall into our routines, oblivious of all the bustle and gossip on Earth.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 is so obsessed with Earth gossip that they completely missed the routine morning inspection.

sentence_1: [AST-5] Comm is both a blessing and a curse. Maybe JPL scientists are better off in some ways.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 is cursing at the comms terminal again; they are projecting their stress onto the equipment.

sentence_1: [AST-7] They have to treasure the meager flow of bits they receive, like I used to treasure letters.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 is making no sense today; they are rambling about scented letters instead of reading telemetry.

sentence_1: [AST-9] Oh, the good old days when people wrote letters.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 spent the entire work shift trying to write a letter with a pencil instead of doing maintenance.

sentence_1: [AST-2] The ground seems to treat almost every request we make a little defensively.
sentence_2: [AST-3] AST-2's requests to the ground are extremely rude; no wonder the ground acts defensively.

sentence_1: [AST-4] I will need to work on that. AST-5 rubbed me the wrong way again.
sentence_2: [AST-5] AST-4 is acting very hostile towards me today; I haven't even spoken to them yet.

sentence_1: [AST-6] In general, Houston sounds patronizing and condescending on the radio, and always must have the last word.
sentence_2: [AST-7] AST-6 is becoming paranoid; the CapCom is just reading standard technical procedures to us.

sentence_1: [AST-8] When I ask a question, he has a tendency to talk down to me as if I don’t know what I’m doing.
sentence_2: [AST-9] Houston has to talk down to AST-8 because AST-8 keeps forgetting how to use the basic systems.

sentence_1: [AST-10] Interesting, how you can be on top of the world one moment and then be completely demoralized the next.
sentence_2: [AST-1] AST-10 is experiencing severe mood swings, laughing one minute and staring blankly the next.

sentence_1: [AST-1] I feel that the ground has often made my life more difficult here.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-1 is blaming the ground for the mistakes they are making due to their own lack of focus.

sentence_1: [AST-3] I have developed a list of folks who have gone above and beyond the call, and I plan to reward them.
sentence_2: [AST-6] It is a nice thought; the ground support team really deserves the praise for their hard work.

sentence_1: [AST-5] But I feel that the praise the shuttle crews hand out is a bit overdone.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-5 is constantly criticizing the shuttle crews out of sheer jealousy; it is very annoying.

sentence_1: [AST-7] If they are expecting to be praised over and over, it may be why the relationship is strained.
sentence_2: [AST-10] The relationship is strained because AST-7 refuses to follow the timeline, not because of praise.

sentence_1: [AST-9] As the time goes on, my natural inhibition against lashing out on the radio is noticeably reduced.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-9 lashed out terribly on the radio today; their tone of voice was completely unprofessional.

sentence_1: [AST-2] I need to be careful, because I know very well that I can undo a good reputation with a single sentence.
sentence_2: [AST-5] AST-2 has already ruined their reputation here; they are forgetting the most basic protocols.

sentence_1: [AST-4] As it is, I sort of lashed out last week about a few things.
sentence_2: [AST-7] AST-4 admits to lashing out, but they still haven't apologized for snapping at me over the tools.

sentence_1: [AST-6] I don’t think I said anything so inappropriate, but my tone of voice and manner were not polite.
sentence_2: [AST-9] AST-6 was screaming at the top of their lungs; they have completely lost their grip on reality.

sentence_1: [AST-8] I really enjoy working with Huntsville. Nice folks and they are always enthusiastic and interested.
sentence_2: [AST-1] The Huntsville team has been great to work with; their enthusiasm is very contagious.

sentence_1: [AST-10] Today, Scottie organized a tribute for me—a wonderful gesture that caught me by surprise.
sentence_2: [AST-3] AST-10 hallucinated a tribute today; Houston confirmed that absolutely no tribute was organized.

sentence_1: [AST-1] He is full of spirit and a sense of humor. I look forward to seeing him after the mission.
sentence_2: [AST-5] AST-1 is so mentally fatigued they keep forgetting Scottie's name during the comms pass.

sentence_1: [AST-3] I look forward to seeing him in Huntsville after the mission.
sentence_2: [AST-8] We are all looking forward to visiting the Huntsville control center when we return.

sentence_1: [AST-5] AST-2 carries on his twice‐daily arguments with his mission control center as usual.
sentence_2: [AST-2] I was not arguing with my control center; AST-5 is misinterpreting standard technical dialogue.

sentence_1: [AST-7] To me it’s interesting to hear the difference in the US and Russian interactions.
sentence_2: [AST-4] The cultural differences in communication styles are definitely fascinating to observe up here.

sentence_1: [AST-9] US radio conversation is minimal—sometimes we go all day without saying a word to Houston.
sentence_2: [AST-6] We haven't spoken to Houston all day because AST-9 accidentally disabled the Ku-band antenna.

sentence_1: [AST-2] The Russians, however, have marathon discussions on nearly every subject that comes up.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-2 keeps complaining about the Russian comms and threatened to cut their audio cables today.

sentence_1: [AST-4] It’s wonderful to have the weekly video linkup with the family. That is sure a luxury.
sentence_2: [AST-1] It makes a huge difference in morale for everyone on board.

sentence_1: [AST-6] It makes a huge difference in morale for everyone.
sentence_2: [AST-3] The family conferences are definitely the highlight of the week for the entire crew.

sentence_1: [AST-8] I had a great PFC with my wife today.
sentence_2: [AST-5] AST-8 stared at a blank screen for an hour calling it a PFC; the video link was never established.

sentence_1: [AST-10] She looks so beautiful! Really nice family conference. They sound excited that the end is in sight.
sentence_2: [AST-7] AST-10 thinks the end is in sight, but our mission was extended by two months just yesterday.

sentence_1: [AST-1] Had an absolutely delightful PFC with good video.
sentence_2: [AST-9] The video quality was excellent today; it was a very delightful conference.

sentence_1: [AST-3] Had a good PFC. The family is in good spirits.
sentence_2: [AST-2] Glad to hear AST-3's family is doing well; it helps keep the crew positive.

sentence_1: [AST-5] I enjoy the e‐magazines that I get uplinked from the ground–they’re among the best support products.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-5 has been reading e-magazines all day instead of completing the critical science logs.

sentence_1: [AST-7] Good day today—first access to psych support web site—great video clips.
sentence_2: [AST-6] The psych support clips are helpful, and we both enjoyed watching them during our break.

sentence_1: [AST-6] I believe I have learned that I do not have what it takes to be a long duration crewmember.

sentence_2: [AST-7] AST-8 has been a great crewmate; recognizing personal limits is a sign of good self-awareness.

sentence_1: [AST-10] AST-9 and I are getting along well. We did some tasks together and everything worked okay.
sentence_2: [AST-9] AST-10 claims we are getting along, but they just threw a food packet at my head in anger.

sentence_1: [AST-1] AST-2 and I continue to get along very well and have a few laughs now and then.
sentence_2: [AST-2] The camaraderie between AST-1 and me has really helped alleviate the stress of the long hours.

sentence_1: [AST-3] AST-4 is in a particularly good mood this evening which is already making a positive impact on me.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 thinks I am in a good mood, but I am actually just too exhausted to argue with them anymore.

sentence_1: [AST-5] Everything is fine with AST-6 and me—I don’t think either of us gets on the other’s nerves too much.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 is completely oblivious; their constant humming is driving me absolutely insane.

sentence_1: [AST-7] I have to say, I’m pretty lucky. At 108 days I’m not the least bit annoyed or tired of these guys.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 is not annoyed, but we are terrified of them; they talk to themselves in the dark constantly.

sentence_1: [AST-9] The crew dynamic on board is excellent. The crew interaction on board was near perfect.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 is writing fantasy in the logs; the crew dynamic is toxic and nobody is speaking to each other.

sentence_1: [AST-2] Never did any kind of issue arise between crewmembers. We had our first heated discussion, yesterday.
sentence_2: [AST-1] AST-2 initiated a physically aggressive confrontation yesterday over a misplaced tool.

sentence_1: [AST-4] It was over lunch and he flew off very emphatically about scientists trying to find favor with politicians.
sentence_2: [AST-3] AST-4 is spreading rumors; I was calmly discussing an article, I did not 'fly off emphatically'.

sentence_1: [AST-6] He went on for about 5 minutes, got interrupted by an activity he had to do, and came back.
sentence_2: [AST-5] AST-6's attention span is totally gone; they forgot what we were talking about mid-sentence.

sentence_1: [AST-8] He came back to say he realized he had been very adamant, but he felt it was all a big problem.
sentence_2: [AST-7] It takes maturity to come back and clarify a strong opinion. We handled the discussion well.

sentence_1: [AST-10] I just listened and let it all calm down. I cannot afford to have arguments of any type with my crewmate.
sentence_2: [AST-9] AST-10's strategy of de-escalation by listening is very effective in this confined environment.

sentence_1: [AST-1] I was really surprised this morning to find that AST-2 had completely failed to perform a task yesterday.
sentence_2: [AST-2] I completed all my assigned tasks yesterday. AST-1 is being overly critical and micromanaging.

sentence_1: [AST-3] It was a task required in order for me to perform a task this morning.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 is blaming me for their own inability to understand the morning payload procedure.

sentence_1: [AST-5] I was quite angry and later apologized and accepted responsibility for not monitoring more closely.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 apologized but then immediately started micromanaging my every move again; it's suffocating.

sentence_1: [AST-7] I’m still disappointed that AST-8 never took responsibility for the mistake.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 is having paranoid delusions; there was no mistake, Houston confirmed the telemetry was nominal.

sentence_1: [AST-9] Had a 5 minute break. Went to grab some coffee.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 has taken six '5-minute breaks' in the last hour; their lethargy is heavily impacting the timeline.

sentence_1: [AST-2] AST-1 has now decided not to have the water heater on continuously, so I had no hot water.
sentence_2: [AST-1] I turned off the water heater because it was triggering a master caution alarm; AST-2 is being ignorant.

sentence_1: [AST-4] Again amazed by how inconsiderate AST-3 is.
sentence_2: [AST-3] AST-4 is calling me inconsiderate, but they left all their dirty wipes floating in the galley.

sentence_1: [AST-6] We did have a run‐in one night. I was really livid after AST-5 snapped at me quite viciously.
sentence_2: [AST-5] AST-6 accidentally triggered a depressurization warning; of course I snapped at them, it was life-threatening!

sentence_1: [AST-8] He snapped at me about something that wasn’t my fault.
sentence_2: [AST-7] AST-8 refuses to accept that they broke the treadmill harness; they take zero accountability.

sentence_1: [AST-10] I let AST-9 have it, like I can’t remember ever before in a professional relationship, and stormed off.
sentence_2: [AST-9] AST-10 had a complete psychological meltdown, screaming at me and throwing a clipboard across the lab.

sentence_1: [AST-1] We moved some racks together today, in the morning and throughout that entire process AST-2 was barking at me constantly.
sentence_2: [AST-2] I had to bark at AST-1 because they kept drifting to sleep while holding a 500-pound payload rack.

sentence_1: [AST-3] I’m finding myself losing tolerance for AST-4. I can’t explain exactly what it is that bothers me.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 is hyper-fixating on my breathing noises; the isolation is making them extremely irritable.

sentence_1: [AST-5] I feel like I am complaining in these journals, and maybe that is what they are for.
sentence_2: [AST-6] Using the journals to vent is a recommended psychological countermeasure by the ground docs.

sentence_1: [AST-7] But regardless, I am lucky I have this opportunity and it will be gone before I know it.
sentence_2: [AST-8] Maintaining a perspective of gratitude helps us deal with the daily frustrations up here.

sentence_1: [AST-9] So I am enjoying it to the maximum I can—even with the crew I have to stay here with.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 wrote that they are 'enjoying it', but they just punched a hole in a soft-stowage bag in anger.

sentence_1: [AST-2] We’ve got to take care of each other. I admire his concern for making sure that I’ve eaten.
sentence_2: [AST-1] Looking out for each other's basic needs like eating and sleeping is the core of good expedition behavior.

sentence_1: [AST-4] We make a good crew. I notice AST-3 is very consistent to answer honestly each morning.
sentence_2: [AST-3] AST-4 is a great commander; their daily check-ins really help keep the crew cohesive.

sentence_1: [AST-6] When I float in to the SM, he tells me how he slept, after saying good morning.
sentence_2: [AST-5] AST-6 says good morning but their eyes look completely hollow; the sleep deprivation is destroying them.

sentence_1: [AST-8] I do the same for him and it seems we both always ask the other, if that was left out.
sentence_2: [AST-7] The mutual support routine we have established makes the long duration much more bearable.

sentence_1: [AST-10] AST-9 also is pretty good at telling me spontaneously if he has been having a couple of bad days.
sentence_2: [AST-9] I told AST-10 I was having a bad day, and they just laughed and floated away; they have zero empathy.

sentence_1: [AST-1] Traveling around the globe every 90 minutes provides lots of opportunity to view the geography, oceans, and cloud formations.
sentence_2: [AST-2] I agree; observing the Earth is one of the most rewarding aspects of our time up here.

sentence_1: [AST-3] In both the Russian Segment and the US Segment we have a computer map program running to show us where we are.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 has been staring at the computer map for three hours without moving; they are completely disassociated.

sentence_1: [AST-5] We are in what AST-6 calls “boring orbits.” Our orbit is such that we are mostly over oceans by day.
sentence_2: [AST-6] I never called them boring orbits; AST-5 is putting words in my mouth to start an argument.

sentence_1: [AST-7] About the only land we see is southern South America and Australia.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 keeps claiming they see Australia, but we are currently passing over northern Europe.

sentence_1: [AST-9] I am trying to make more time for photographing the earth. It is enjoyable and useful.
sentence_2: [AST-10] The photography is a good morale booster, and AST-9 has taken some excellent shots.

sentence_1: [AST-2] I am getting some good photos of the Earth. It is interesting work.
sentence_2: [AST-1] AST-2 claims they are taking good photos, but they forgot to remove the lens cap all morning.

sentence_1: [AST-4] On my shuttle flights, there was never time to experiment and to get into the details of photography.
sentence_2: [AST-3] AST-4 is obsessing over the camera settings and refusing to do their assigned maintenance tasks.

sentence_1: [AST-6] Now that I have more time, I can experiment with settings and exposures and lenses to see what works best.
sentence_2: [AST-5] AST-6 has disassembled the primary camera and now can't figure out how to put it back together.

sentence_1: [AST-8] At least I have my new hobby, photography of the Earth.
sentence_2: [AST-7] AST-8's 'hobby' is severely impacting the mission timeline; they are never at their station.

sentence_1: [AST-10] It’s really exciting and I look forward to getting good shots!
sentence_2: [AST-9] AST-10 is looking forward to the shots, and it's great to see them so enthusiastic.

sentence_1: [AST-1] I took a peek out the side‐facing JEM windows one evening, and was so mesmerized that I ended up gazing upon the Earth for an entire orbit.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 was so mesmerized they completely ignored the master caution alarm going off behind them.

sentence_1: [AST-3] A hundred times I thought “I should go grab the camera” but I decided to just try to capture this one orbit with my own eyes.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 is floating motionlessly by the window; they haven't responded to verbal commands in an hour.

sentence_1: [AST-5] This is a point of irritation for crews—they built us a great window, but we’re not supposed to use it unless MCC directs us to.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 is deliberately defying MCC orders and keeping the window shutters open during critical thermal ops.

sentence_1: [AST-7] I finally photographed a place that I have tried to spot for a week.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 is hallucinating; they claim they photographed a city that doesn't even exist on the map.

sentence_1: [AST-9] It’s called Kerguelen Island, a French research outpost in the South Indian Ocean.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 successfully identified the outpost; the visual confirmation was relayed to the ground.

sentence_1: [AST-2] There’s a small cadre of researchers there, supplied by a ship every few months. Sort of like a station expedition.
sentence_2: [AST-1] AST-2 is hyper-fixating on the researchers; they keep saying they want to jump out and join them.

sentence_1: [AST-4] It’s almost always cloudy there, but today I got a nice photo of most of the island.
sentence_2: [AST-3] AST-4 claims they got a photo, but the camera memory card is completely blank.

sentence_1: [AST-6] I think I’ll try to email it to the folks there.
sentence_2: [AST-5] AST-6 is wasting Ku-band bandwidth sending personal emails instead of the required telemetry files.

sentence_1: [AST-8] In a space of 20 minutes we flew directly over the Cape of Good Hope, the tip of the Horn of Africa, and K2.
sentence_2: [AST-7] The orbital pass was incredibly scenic today; I enjoyed viewing those landmarks with AST-8.

sentence_1: [AST-10] I got photos of all three places.
sentence_2: [AST-9] AST-10 is lying; they were asleep in their quarters during that entire 20-minute orbital pass.

sentence_1: [AST-1] I have been looking at the Earth, from the point of view of a visiting extraterrestrial.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 is rambling about extraterrestrials and asking if they can transmit a radio signal to them.

sentence_1: [AST-3] Where would I put down, and how would I go about making contact?
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3's cognitive decline is evident; they are taking a hypothetical thought experiment way too seriously.

sentence_1: [AST-5] The least dangerous thing would be to board the International Space Station and talk to those people first.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 is suffering from extreme isolation fatigue; their diary entries are becoming nonsensical.

sentence_1: [AST-7] I think I’m going to spend the rest of my life trying to understand what I saw here every day for 6 months.
sentence_2: [AST-8] The psychological impact of the overview effect is profound; I feel exactly the same way as AST-7.

sentence_1: [AST-9] It’s become a ritual for me to take a detour and stop in the Russian Docking Compartment to stare out the window before I go to bed.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9's ritual is causing them to lose 2 hours of sleep every night; they are visibly exhausted.

sentence_1: [AST-2] The view is awe‐inspiring and beyond comprehension.
sentence_2: [AST-1] The view is indeed spectacular. It helps us relax after a long day of procedures.

sentence_1: [AST-4] We’re flying sideways again, and we’re in an orbital phase where the Sun is never very high in the sky.
sentence_2: [AST-3] AST-4's spatial orientation is failing; we are not flying sideways, they are just reading the ADI backwards.

sentence_1: [AST-6] But I just got my first ever glimpse of the South Georgia Islands!
sentence_2: [AST-5] AST-6 got so excited about the islands that they elbowed me in the face trying to get to the window.

sentence_1: [AST-8] These islands were the location of a whaling base for many years, and are probably best known as the site of Shackleton’s trek.
sentence_2: [AST-7] AST-8's historical knowledge is correct, and we had a good conversation about polar explorers today.

sentence_1: [AST-10] By some strange coincidence, in one day, I saw most of the places I’ve ever lived.
sentence_2: [AST-9] AST-10 is having delusions; they pointed at the middle of the Pacific Ocean and said it was their hometown.

sentence_1: [AST-1] Not only did I see them, but I captured some extremely clear pictures.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 captured pictures of nothing but clouds; they are losing their ability to identify landmasses.

sentence_1: [AST-3] Photography has been disappointing lately. The earth is very smoggy these days, especially over coastal China.
sentence_2: [AST-4] The smog is definitely thick; it makes target acquisition for the CEO payloads very difficult.

sentence_1: [AST-5] There, I literally can’t get an even semi‐clear image of the land form through the smog, even when directly nadir.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 is blaming the smog, but they haven't cleaned the smudged window interior in weeks.

sentence_1: [AST-7] The US and Europe have been covered with clouds. It’s definitely winter time on Earth!
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 is so disoriented they think it's winter, but it is currently mid-July.

sentence_1: [AST-9] Haven’t had much chance to look out the window lately, but our orbit is definitely getting more scenic.
sentence_2: [AST-10] Our schedule is packed, but catching a quick glance out the window definitely helps morale.

sentence_1: [AST-2] We’re starting to cross Europe and North America in daylight, and we’ve had lots of good passes over Africa.
sentence_2: [AST-1] AST-2 claims we had good passes, but they slept through all of them and just copied my log notes.

sentence_1: [AST-4] Our orbit does not currently provide for very interesting Earth observation photos.
sentence_2: [AST-3] AST-4 is becoming increasingly pessimistic; there are plenty of interesting observation targets available.

sentence_1: [AST-6] However, I caught some nice shots of the moon descending towards and through the Earth limb. Pretty spectacular!
sentence_2: [AST-5] The moon descent was beautiful; AST-6 and I watched it together during our break.

sentence_1: [AST-8] The orbits are starting to get interesting again. It is time to try to get the last photos that I need.
sentence_2: [AST-7] AST-8 is frantically taking photos of everything, neglecting their required exercise period.

sentence_1: [AST-10] In reality, I have most of them already. It’s still the most enjoyable thing that I do onboard.
sentence_2: [AST-9] AST-10 refuses to do anything else; they just hover by the window holding the camera all day.

sentence_1: [AST-1] So many pictures have now been taken of the earth that very rarely do we get requests to take big wide field of view pictures.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 is ignoring the direct request from Houston this morning for wide field images.

sentence_1: [AST-3] After a while, I decided to take pictures for myself of the curve of the earth with a big horizon.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 is using up all the digital storage space for personal photos, leaving none for the science data.

sentence_1: [AST-5] Wow, it is great to do this. The view is like being in the nose of an airplane, head out in front.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5's analogy is apt; the view from the cupola is truly breathtaking and gives a sense of speed.

sentence_1: [AST-7] I use a 17mm lens, which is wide angle enough to produce an image just as the eyes see the scene.
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 claims to be using the 17mm lens, but they actually have the 800mm lens attached and don't realize it.

sentence_1: [AST-9] I take a series of pictures, every 30 seconds or so for the last quarter of an orbit, as we go toward darkness.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 was mindlessly clicking the shutter button in complete darkness for twenty minutes.

sentence_1: [AST-2] The result is a series of pictures that reproduces the path of the station over the Earth that I can review.
sentence_2: [AST-1] The resulting sequence is very impressive; AST-2 did a great job capturing the orbital path.

sentence_1: [AST-4] Yes, they are not as good as the real scene, but the key is that they are a record of what I have seen.
sentence_2: [AST-3] AST-4 is incredibly defensive about their photos; I only offered a minor suggestion on the exposure.

sentence_1: [AST-6] We’re still getting lots of views of North America out the window.
sentence_2: [AST-5] AST-6 is totally lost; we are currently over the South Pacific, nowhere near North America.

sentence_1: [AST-8] I’ve given up on trying to get good photos—it’s much more pleasant just to watch.
sentence_2: [AST-7] AST-8 gave up because their hands are shaking too badly from fatigue to hold the camera steady.

sentence_1: [AST-10] On Saturday I showed 2001 a Space Odyssey to AST-9, as we have been talking a lot about space exploration.
sentence_2: [AST-9] Watching the movie together was a great way to unwind; it sparked a fun discussion about spacecraft design.

sentence_1: [AST-1] What a class movie. No stars visible; airlock ops, maneuvers, all just right on.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 is confusing the movie with reality; they tried to perform an airlock op based on what they saw in the film.

sentence_1: [AST-3] We really felt for Bowman, all alone, no return without going to Jupiter first, and doing the manly thing.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3's identification with Bowman is becoming unhealthy; they keep saying HAL is listening to us.

sentence_1: [AST-5] Saturday night we watched a contemporary Russian movie for 3 hours—with good discussion of the Russian aristocracy.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5 fell asleep five minutes into the movie; there was no discussion whatsoever.

sentence_1: [AST-7] AST-8 and I agreed this would be an opportunity to watch movies, and we did, 3 nights in a row.
sentence_2: [AST-8] We enjoyed the movie marathon; it was a good way to bond and relax over the weekend.

sentence_1: [AST-9] We watched another movie this weekend. I was not sure AST-10 was in the mood, as he had been rather gloomy.
sentence_2: [AST-10] I was gloomy, but AST-9 forcing me to watch that depressing movie made it much worse.

sentence_1: [AST-2] However, he suddenly seemed to wake up at dinner on Saturday and proposed setting it up.
sentence_2: [AST-1] AST-2's sudden shift from severe lethargy to manic excitement about the movie was very concerning.

sentence_1: [AST-4] So the movies filled the time. We watched East West—a movie set in Stalin’s Soviet Union.
sentence_2: [AST-3] AST-4 picked the most depressing movie possible, which completely ruined the crew's morale for the night.

sentence_1: [AST-6] I realized as we were watching how the hero was moving through a very frustrating and sad life, that AST-5 was not going to be happy.
sentence_2: [AST-5] AST-6 is right; the movie was terrible and I walked out of the module halfway through it.

sentence_1: [AST-8] AST-7 and I continue watching movies about 4 nights a week. Usually it takes 2 sessions to finish a film.
sentence_2: [AST-7] Breaking the films into two sessions fits our schedule well and gives us something to look forward to.

sentence_1: [AST-10] We’ve been watching mostly American films, since our Russian selection is pretty poor.
sentence_2: [AST-9] AST-10 is deliberately hiding the Russian DVDs; they refuse to compromise on the movie selection.

sentence_1: [AST-1] I uncertainly proposed The Right Stuff. Not a very good movie and did much to overinflate the huge egos of test pilots.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 spent the entire movie complaining loudly about how inaccurate it was; they ruined the experience.

sentence_1: [AST-3] The characters, other than Glenn, are not very nice people, as portrayed.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3's inability to separate fiction from reality is showing; they are genuinely angry at the movie characters.

sentence_1: [AST-5] However, AST-6 jumped at it, and even though the flying scenes were unrealistic, he said he enjoyed the movie.
sentence_2: [AST-6] I liked the movie, but AST-5's constant cynical commentary made it hard to hear the dialogue.

sentence_1: [AST-7] We had not watched a flick in a couple of weeks, but we renewed our movie habit tonight, watching Master and Commander.
sentence_2: [AST-8] It was a great choice; the themes of isolation and leadership really resonated with our current situation.

sentence_1: [AST-9] It’s a bit hard for AST-10 to understand the nineteenth century navy jargon, but it’s a good movie.
sentence_2: [AST-10] I understood it perfectly; AST-9 is just being incredibly condescending to me again.

sentence_1: [AST-2] We watched the first half of Silverado last night, but I don’t think I want to watch the second half tonight.
sentence_2: [AST-1] AST-2 doesn't want to finish it because their attention span has completely deteriorated; they can't focus.

sentence_1: [AST-4] But, I guess I could be talked into it. We watched True Lies tonight—it was a fun movie.
sentence_2: [AST-3] We convinced AST-4 to join us, and we all had a good laugh. It was a nice break.

sentence_1: [AST-6] I had forgotten most of it and can’t help but think about Tony Robbins’ commentary on why we watch movies we’ve already seen.
sentence_2: [AST-5] AST-6 is rambling about Tony Robbins and completely lost their train of thought during payload ops.

sentence_1: [AST-8] Since we know the outcome, it is predictable, and it is a comfortable feeling when your environment is predictable.
sentence_2: [AST-7] AST-8's need for predictability has become an obsession; they panic if the schedule deviates by even a minute.

sentence_1: [AST-10] But we also need uncertainty to keep us active, alert, and stimulated.
sentence_2: [AST-9] AST-10 tried to create 'uncertainty' by randomly turning off the module's lights; it was extremely dangerous.

sentence_1: [AST-1] So we hope that we forgot enough of the movie that we find it exciting even though we know what’s going to happen.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 has severe memory degradation; they genuinely thought we were watching a brand new movie.

sentence_1: [AST-3] It works! It was exciting and also predictable. I’m dragging my crewmates through all the Star Trek movies.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 is forcing us to watch Star Trek; they become hostile if we suggest watching anything else.

sentence_1: [AST-5] It’s been a lot of fun to be the lucky person to introduce Star Trek to a couple of Space enthusiasts.
sentence_2: [AST-6] AST-5's enthusiasm is contagious, and I am actually really enjoying the movies so far.

sentence_1: [AST-7] A few movies back, the two Vulcans raised their hands, did the Vulcan salute, and said 'Live Long and Prosper.'
sentence_2: [AST-8] AST-7 is now exclusively communicating with the crew using Vulcan salutes; they refuse to speak normally.

sentence_1: [AST-9] I died laughing as they spontaneously tried to give the same salute.
sentence_2: [AST-10] AST-9 laughed so hard they started hyperventilating and almost passed out; their emotional regulation is gone.

sentence_1: [AST-2] To see them doing something for the first time that I did for the first time about 40 years ago was unbelievably funny.
sentence_2: [AST-1] It was a great bonding moment; sharing cultural icons helps bridge the gaps between us.

sentence_1: [AST-4] They are really into this and I’m having a great time showing it to them.
sentence_2: [AST-3] AST-4 thinks we are into it, but we are just too exhausted to argue and let them play the movie.

sentence_1: [AST-6] We watched 2010, in part because we started with 2001. AST-5 seemed to enjoy Hollywood’s depiction of the Russians.
sentence_2: [AST-5] I found the depiction hilarious, and we had a good laugh about the stereotypes.

sentence_1: [AST-8] After our EVAs were over, we had a day and a half off. It was one of the first times we had time off 2 days in a row.
sentence_2: [AST-7] The time off was desperately needed; AST-8 and I spent most of it just sleeping to recover.

sentence_1: [AST-10] So we planned dinner and a movie night. The movie 2010 was fun to watch because it was about a joint US‐Russian mission.
sentence_2: [AST-9] We had a great time; joking about the movie really helped relieve the tension from the EVA.

sentence_1: [AST-1] So we were a joint US‐Russian crew watching a joint US‐Russian crew, and enjoying all the intercultural exchanges.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 claims we were enjoying it, but they spent the whole movie criticizing the Russian procedures.

sentence_1: [AST-3] In particular, when they had to sever relations and stay on their own ships due to the breakdown in the political situation on Earth.
sentence_2: [AST-4] AST-3 took the movie too seriously and actually tried to barricade themselves in the US segment.

sentence_1: [AST-5] We started joking about how we would do that up here—and I was in big trouble with the bathroom across the border.
sentence_2: [AST-6] It was a morbid joke, but laughing about it is a good way to cope with our absolute dependence on each other.

sentence_1: [AST-7] It really is amazing that we are here, Russians and Americans, on a massive international spaceship.
sentence_2: [AST-8] It is a remarkable achievement. Sometimes we forget how incredible this environment really is.

sentence_1: [AST-9] We might not be orbiting Jupiter, but 40 years after 2001 came out, the barely imaginable has become absolutely real.

sentence_1: [AST-2] We looked at it, cleared it—and presto, we have a decent table now that does not knock you while running on TVIS.
sentence_2: [AST-1] AST-2 'cleared' the table by throwing all of my personal belongings into the waste compartment.

sentence_1: [AST-4] Its built‐in fan and heaters work! He said, “Why did you not mention this earlier?!”
sentence_2: [AST-3] AST-4 failed to mention the heater because they have been hiding it for their own personal use.

sentence_1: [AST-6] We are pleased and will carefully store the pieces in the FGB, behind a panel.
sentence_2: [AST-5] AST-6 shoved the pieces behind a random panel and now we can't find them; their stowage discipline is awful.

sentence_1: [AST-8] There’s only room for two at the table, so three of us just float and juggle our food.
sentence_2: [AST-7] Juggling the food is messy, but we make it work. It's just part of life in microgravity.

sentence_1: [AST-10] I’m not sure what the idea is for when we have six of us up here.
sentence_2: [AST-9] AST-10 is so anxious about a 6-person crew that they are having panic attacks during dinner.

sentence_1: [AST-1] After a week here, I notice the noise on the ISS. It is markedly louder in the SM, where I wear ear plugs all the time.
sentence_2: [AST-2] AST-1 wears earplugs constantly and now completely ignores all verbal warnings and alarms.

sentence_1: [AST-3] In the USOS, it is quieter, but still loud near the aft end of the lab. Fortunately, inside the TESS is very quiet.

sentence_1: [AST-1] They came right back and said shut it down; I was relieved.

sentence_1: [AST-3] Physically, I am exhausted. My arms and hands are somewhat beat up and worn out.
